title: Permanent relocation go-folder
summary: A worst-case routine for preparing records for the case where return home is no longer safe.
content: Permanent relocation go-folder is designed for conditions where normal services, internet, transport, or outside help may be delayed. The practical goal is preparing records for the case where return home is no longer safe. Keep the routine visible, assign one calm recorder, protect vulnerable people first, and change the plan only when conditions or official instructions clearly change.
materials: Notebook or board. Context: “Permanent relocation go-folder”.
Marker or pencil. Context: “Permanent relocation go-folder”.
Tape or labels. Context: “Permanent relocation go-folder”.
Local map or contact list — for «Permanent relocation go-folder»
steps: Write the exact trigger for starting permanent relocation go-folder.
List people, supplies, hazards, and safe locations before action starts. Apply it specifically within the “Permanent relocation go-folder” procedure.
Use the routine to focus on preparing records for the case where return home is no longer safe.
Check vulnerable people first and document unmet needs. Apply it specifically within the “Permanent relocation go-folder” procedure.
Review the plan at fixed times or when official instructions change. Apply it specifically within the “Permanent relocation go-folder” procedure.
warnings: Follow local authorities and qualified responders whenever they are available. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Permanent relocation go-folder”.
Do not improvise with weapons, explosives, dangerous chemicals, medical dosing, or unsafe building entry. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Permanent relocation go-folder”.
Do not let curiosity, filming, rumors, or pride replace distance, shelter, evacuation, hygiene, and records. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Permanent relocation go-folder”.
common_mistakes: Starting too late because the first signs were ignored while carrying out “Permanent relocation go-folder”
Letting many people make conflicting decisions at the same time while carrying out “Permanent relocation go-folder”
Using memory instead of a visible written record while carrying out “Permanent relocation go-folder”
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Permanent relocation go-folder with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Permanent relocation go-folder if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Permanent relocation go-folder and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Permanent relocation go-folder or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Permanent relocation go-folder are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores clear records, verified messages, offline copies, and review times and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Permanent relocation go-folder”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Permanent relocation go-folder as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Permanent relocation go-folder when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Permanent relocation go-folder.
short_term: For the first hours of Permanent relocation go-folder, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Permanent relocation go-folder remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Permanent relocation go-folder fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Permanent relocation go-folder to the real environment: check clear records, verified messages, offline copies, and review times, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
